content.page_title: Best Bariatric Surgeons in Montreal, Quebec (2026)
content.meta_description: Find top-rated bariatric surgeons in Montreal, QC. Expert weight loss surgery options, costs, and insurance coverage for Canadian patients.
content.h1: Best Bariatric Surgeons in Montreal, Quebec
content.intro: If you're considering bariatric surgery in Montreal, you're taking a serious step toward lasting weight loss and better health. This guide walks you through Montreal's leading bariatric surgeons, what to expect, costs, and how provincial insurance covers these life-changing procedures.
content.local_context: Montreal is home to several world-class medical centers and bariatric specialists. Quebec's healthcare system covers medically necessary bariatric surgery through RAMQ (Régie de l'assurance maladie du Québec) when strict criteria are met. The city has seen growing demand for these services as obesit

top_picks.name: Montreal Bariatric Surgery Centre (MBSC)
top_picks.tagline: Comprehensive bariatric care with experienced surgeons
top_picks.description: MBSC is a dedicated bariatric facility in downtown Montreal offering gastric bypass, sleeve gastrectomy, and lap-band procedures. They provide pre- and post-operative support with nutritionists and psychologists on staff. Surgeons are fellowship-trained and have performed over 5,000 procedures.
top_picks.best_for: Patients seeking comprehensive, covered bariatric care with strong post-op support

top_picks.name: Wharton Medical Clinic – Montreal
top_picks.tagline: Canada-wide bariatric expertise with local presence
top_picks.description: Wharton Medical is a nationally recognized bariatric clinic with a Montreal location offering consultation, pre-surgery assessments, and post-operative follow-up. They work with local surgeons and emphasize personalized weight loss plans before and after surgery.
top_picks.price_range: $300–600 for private consultation; surgery costs vary
top_picks.best_for: Patients who want comprehensive pre- and post-op coaching from a trusted national provider

top_picks.name: Centre Hospitalier de l'Université de Montréal (CHUM) – Bariatric Surgery
top_picks.tagline: University hospital bariatric program with research backing
top_picks.description: CHUM's bariatric surgery program is part of a major teaching hospital. Surgeons are faculty members with extensive research and clinical experience. They offer all modern bariatric procedures and are involved in clinical outcomes research.
top_picks.best_for: Patients seeking cutting-edge, research-backed bariatric care through the public system

top_picks.name: Ideal Protein Montreal – Bariatric Support
top_picks.tagline: Medically supervised weight loss and bariatric prep
top_picks.description: Ideal Protein offers a medically supervised weight loss program designed to help patients prepare for bariatric surgery or achieve results without surgery. Montreal clinics are staffed by nurses and nutritionists who coordinate with local surgeons.
top_picks.best_for: Patients who want structured medical support before or instead of bariatric surgery

top_picks.name: Dr. Jean-Marc Lavoie – Private Bariatric Practice
top_picks.tagline: Experienced private bariatric surgeon with personalized care
top_picks.description: Dr. Lavoie is a fellowship-trained bariatric surgeon with 20+ years of experience. His private practice in Montreal offers consultations, surgery, and comprehensive follow-up. He specializes in sleeve gastrectomy and revision cases.
top_picks.best_for: Patients seeking premium private bariatric surgery with a renowned local surgeon

top_picks.name: Herbal Magic – Montreal Weight Loss Centers
top_picks.tagline: Natural weight loss support before or after bariatric surgery
top_picks.description: Herbal Magic operates multiple Montreal locations offering natural supplement-based weight loss programs. While not a bariatric surgery provider, they support patients preparing for or recovering from surgery with nutrition and natural health products.
top_picks.best_for: Patients looking for natural supplement support alongside bariatric surgery planning

what_to_look_for: Surgeon credentials: Look for fellowship training in bariatric surgery and membership in the Canadian Society for Bariatric Surgery (CSBS).
Hospital affiliation: Ensure the surgeon operates at an accredited hospital (CHUM, Jewish General, Royal Victoria, or private surgical centers).
Patient support: Choose a clinic offering pre-op counseling, nutritionist support, and long-term post-op follow-up to maximize success.
Insurance coverage: Confirm whether RAMQ covers the procedure or if you'll pay privately ($8,000–20,000 CAD for surgery alone).
Experience and outcomes: Ask about complication rates, revision rates, and average weight loss results from their patients.
Communication style: Pick a surgeon and team you feel comfortable with—bariatric surgery is a partnership requiring ongoing communication.
content.average_cost_context: In Quebec, RAMQ covers bariatric surgery when medically necessary (BMI >40 or >35 with comorbidities). Private surgery costs range from $8,000–20,000 CAD depending on the procedure and facility. Pre-op and post-op support services add $200–600 per consultation.
content.insurance_notes: RAMQ (Quebec's provincial health insurance) covers bariatric surgery for eligible patients, but requires a referral from your family doctor and approval based on strict BMI and health criteria. Wait times for public surgery are typically 6–12 months. Private surgery is not covered by RAMQ but may be covered by some employer group plans. Nutritional follow-up is not always covered and may require private payment.

faqs.question: Does RAMQ cover bariatric surgery in Montreal?
faqs.answer: Yes, RAMQ covers bariatric surgery if you meet strict criteria: BMI >40, or BMI >35 with obesity-related health conditions (diabetes, sleep apnea, etc.). You need a referral from your family doctor and approval from a bariatric surgery center. Public wait times are typically 6–12 months.

faqs.question: How long does it take to see a bariatric surgeon in Montreal?
faqs.answer: Public system (RAMQ-covered): 6–12 months or longer. Private surgeons: 2–8 weeks depending on availability. Consultations with nutritionists or weight loss clinics can often be scheduled within 2–4 weeks.

faqs.question: What's the difference between gastric bypass and sleeve gastrectomy?
faqs.answer: Gastric bypass reduces stomach size and reroutes the small intestine, limiting both intake and absorption. Sleeve gastrectomy removes about 75% of the stomach, reducing intake only. Bypass typically results in more weight loss but has more nutritional risks. Sleeve is simpler and has fewer complications.

faqs.question: How much weight can I expect to lose after bariatric surgery?
faqs.answer: Most patients lose 50–70% of excess weight within 18–24 months. Results vary based on surgery type, diet, exercise, and individual metabolism. Gastric bypass often yields more weight loss than sleeve gastrectomy. Long-term success depends on lifestyle changes.

faqs.question: Are there nutritional risks after bariatric surgery?
faqs.answer: Yes. Gastric bypass patients risk deficiencies in vitamin B12, iron, calcium, and folate. Sleeve gastrectomy has lower risks but still requires lifelong monitoring. Regular follow-up with a nutritionist and blood tests are essential. Most surgeons recommend lifelong multivitamin supplementation.

faqs.question: Can I get bariatric surgery privately in Montreal if I don't qualify for RAMQ?
faqs.answer: Yes. Private bariatric surgeons in Montreal perform surgery for patients who don't meet RAMQ criteria or prefer faster access. Costs range from $8,000–20,000 CAD depending on the procedure. Some employer group health plans may cover private bariatric surgery—check your policy.

faqs.question: What should I do to prepare for a bariatric surgery consultation in Montreal?
faqs.answer: Gather your medical history, current medications, and any recent lab work. Write down your weight loss goals and health concerns. Have a referral from your family doctor ready if pursuing public surgery. Be honest about your diet, exercise habits, and previous weight loss attempts.

faqs.question: How long is the recovery after bariatric surgery?
faqs.answer: Most patients return to light activities within 2–3 weeks and resume normal activities within 4–6 weeks. Full recovery takes 8–12 weeks. You'll have multiple follow-up appointments with your surgeon, nutritionist, and may need blood work to monitor for deficiencies.
